quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 14 Mixed Review
quiz.subtitle: Unit 14 Quiz 11: Matrices & Determinants — Challenging Problems (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Unit 14: Matrices & Determinants — High-End Mixed Review

questions.question: Find the inverse of $A = \begin{bmatrix} 3 & 4 \\ 1 & 1 \end{bmatrix}$ and use it to solve $AX = \begin{bmatrix} 10 \\ 3 \end{bmatrix}$.
Find the determinant of $\begin{bmatrix} 2 & 1 & 0 \\ -1 & 3 & 2 \\ 4 & 1 & 1 \end{bmatrix}$ by expanding along the first row.
Use Cramer's Rule to solve for $y$: $3x + 5y = 11$, $2x - y = 3$.
Rotate the point $(3, -2)$ by $90^{\circ}$ counterclockwise using $R = \begin{bmatrix} 0 & -1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{bmatrix}$.
A point $(4, 1)$ is reflected across the $x$-axis, then dilated by factor $2$. Find the final coordinates.
Use $E = \begin{bmatrix} 2 & 3 \\ 1 & 2 \end{bmatrix}$ and $E^{-1} = \begin{bmatrix} 2 & -3 \\ -1 & 2 \end{bmatrix}$. Encode 'AB' (A=1, B=2) and decode to verify.

Product X: 3 hrs labor, 2 units material. Product Y: 1 hr labor, 4 units material. Use $R = \begin{bmatrix} 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 4 \end{bmatrix}$ and production $P = \begin{bmatrix} 10 \\ 5 \end{bmatrix}$. Find total resources.
Find $k$ such that $\begin{bmatrix} k & 2 \\ 3 & 6 \end{bmatrix}$ is singular (has no inverse).

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered Unit 14: matrix operations, multiplication, inverses, determinants, Cramer's Rule, geometric transformations, cryptography, and real-world applications.
scoreMessages.70: Strong work! Key areas to review: (1) Matrix multiplication: rows of first dot columns of second, (2) $A^{-1} = \frac{1}{\det}\begin{bmatrix} d & -b \\ -c & a \end{bmatrix}$, (3) Cramer's Rule: $x = D_x/D$, (4) Rotation $90^{\circ}$ CCW: $(x,y) \to (-y,x)$.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort on challenging material. Focus on: (1) Scalar multiplication before addition/subtraction, (2) Determinant of 3×3 using expansion by minors, (3) Encoding: $C = EM$, decoding: $M = E^{-1}C$, (4) Singular matrix: $\det = 0$.
scoreMessages.0: Review Unit 14 fundamentals: (1) Matrix addition/subtraction: same dimensions, element-wise, (2) Multiplication: $(a \times b)(b \times c) = a \times c$, dot product of row and column, (3) $A^{-1} = \frac{1}{ad-bc}\begin{bmatrix} d & -b \\ -c & a \end{bmatrix}$, (4) $\det(A) = ad-bc$ for 2×2, expand by minors for 3×3, (5) Cramer's Rule: $x = D_x/D$, $y = D_y/D$.
